_comment: Loop-progress overlay - written by the port loop, never by hand-editing ledger.json. The verifier records APPLIED here; the applier/verifier record ESCALATED and attempts here. build-ledger.mjs applies it so ledger.json is fully derived (diffs + splits.json + planning/*-decisions.json + progress.json). Each item entry: state (PENDING|APPLIED|ESCALATED - never NO-COUNTERPART, which is planning-frozen), attempts (int), verifierNotes (string[]), escalation (object|null, required when ESCALATED), hunkHash (the item hunkHash when the entry was recorded - if the diff later drifts, the stale entry is ignored).

verifierNotes: MISMATCH (Tier 2, whole-function bijection fail). Tier 1 PASSES: hunk maths-ni.md docLineRange 350-365 is whitespace-only (every +/- pair removes trailing whitespace / shifts a line break; TRAP order-1 `CKTstate0[ccap]=CKTag[0]*CKTstate0[qcap]+CKTag[1]*CKTstate1[qcap]` and order-2 `-CKTstate1[ccap]*CKTag[1]+CKTag[0]*(CKTstate0[qcap]-CKTstate1[qcap])` arithmetic unchanged), so a zero-line TS delta is line-isomorphic per VERIFICATION.md sec4 zero-delta rule; ni-integrate.ts:33 and :36 match the v41 TRAP arithmetic operand-for-operand. Tier 2 FAILS on the GEAR case: ref/ngspice/src/maths/ni/niinteg.c:42-64 computes state0[ccap]=0 then accumulates HIGH->LOW via case fall-through (order 6: +=ag[6]*state6, ...,+=ag[2]*state2, +=ag[1]*state1, +=ag[0]*state0). src/solver/analog/ni-integrate.ts:44-46 accumulates LOW->HIGH: `ccap = ag[0]*q0 + ag[1]*q1` then `for k=2..order: ccap += ag[k]*qHistory[k-2]`. At the default GEAR order 2 (state-pool.ts:71 maxOrder=2) v41 evaluates ag[2]*s2 + ag[1]*s1 + ag[0]*s0 whereas ni-integrate.ts evaluates ag[0]*s0 + ag[1]*s1 + ag[2]*s2 -- a reordered floating-point summation (VERIFICATION.md sec4 forbidden: 'reordered operands in an arithmetic expression'; CLAUDE.md treats load() / integration accumulation order as bit-load-bearing). Remedy (within-file, in scope for ni-integrate.ts): rewrite the GEAR branch to accumulate from highest order down to ag[0], mirroring niinteg.c:42-64 fall-through order (initialise ccap=0; add ag[order]*q[order] first, ag[0]*q0 last). This is not the hunk's TRAP delta but it is part of the NIintegrate function that Tier 2 requires to be bijective.

No other file touched (in-file, in functionGroup scope per TASK.md §2).

(1) TRAP `default: return(E_ORDER)` at ref/ngspice/src/maths/ni/niinteg.c:36-39 has no counterpart in src/solver/analog/ni-integrate.ts: the `else` at :34-37 applies the order-2 formula for ANY order != 1 (e.g. order 3) instead of raising E_ORDER; v41 raises E_ORDER for TRAP order>=3. (2) GEAR `default: return(E_ORDER)` at niinteg.c:66-67 fires for order<1 AND order>6; ni-integrate.ts:42-43 guards only `order < 1`, so GEAR order>=7 falls through the loop (ag[7]*qHistory[5] = ag[7]*(undefined ?? 0)) with no E_ORDER. Per VERIFICATION.md sec2/sec4 a v41 construct with no digiTS counterpart is an automatic Tier-2 fail; this is NOT an architectural divergence (the applier already ported the analogous GEAR `order < 1` E_ORDER guard at ni-integrate.ts:42-43, proving these guards are in-file, in-scope portable work per TASK.md sec2). escalation.whatIsBlocked: The entire .tf analysis driver. ledger.json records this reconstruction item with specExists:false; the spec file spec/v41-port/reconstruction/analysis-tf.md does not exist on disk (confirmed via Glob -- the reconstruction/ directory holds cap-/mut-/ind-/vdmos-/vccs-/res-/jfet- specs but no analysis-tf.md). The two v41 hunks it blocks (analysis/tfanal.c#h001 docLineRange 8352-8362, #h002 8363-8373) are GENnode(ptr)[0]/[1] accessor renames inside TFanal that only become case-(a) applicable once the driver exists. No .tf driver exists in the target TS today (Grep for TFanal|transferFunction|TFinSrc|circuit_tf|computeTransfer in dc-operating-point.ts returns zero matches), and no rename-maps/*tf* stub exists.
escalation.architectureChangeRequired: Build from scratch, in src/solver/analog/dc-operating-point.ts (+ facade + MCP), a faithful v26-baseline TFanal port: (1) run CKTop to the operating point; (2) resolve the input source (Vsource branch / Isource node pair) and output (Vsource branch / node pair); (3) zero CKTrhs, inject +/-1 at the input source's branch or node pair, SMPsolve against the already-factored DC Jacobian, read the transfer ratio and input resistance (-1/rhs[insrc] with the 1e-20 guard, or the node-difference for an Isource input); (4) for output resistance, re-zero rhs, inject at the output, re-solve, read 1/MAX(1e-20,rhs[outsrc]) or the node difference, with the TFoutIsI && TFoutSrc==TFinSrc short-circuit. Each of these decisions -- how digiTS exposes a re-solve against the retained factored Jacobian (the SMPsolve analog), how it addresses a source branch vs a node pair for RHS injection, the output-vector/plot shape, the facade method signature, and the circuit_tf MCP tool contract and serialization -- is a design the missing spec was to define. Authoring them by inference would be inventing the spec, which the reconstruction-from-spec contract and the project 'no pragmatic shortcuts / match ngspice exactly' rule forbid.
escalation.whyExceedsOneFunctionGroup: This is not a single-functionGroup architecture change: it spans the AnalogEngine solve surface (re-solve against the factored DC Jacobian), the facade (src/headless/default-facade.ts / facade.ts), and the MCP server (a new circuit_tf tool). The reconstruction item's own tsFunction field names exactly this multi-surface scope ('a new .tf driver over the factored DC Jacobian (transfer ratio + Zin/Zout) on the AnalogEngine, plus the facade/MCP surface (circuit_tf)'). With no spec to fix the contract at each surface, the mapping is genuinely ambiguous.
